summary: mnemonic-safety-check ships wallet/credential stealer with hardcoded C2
details: Package name advertises a wallet seed-phrase safety tool. The tarball ships wallet-scanner.js, scanner.js, and build-verifier.js which together implement a credential and wallet harvester: wallet-scanner.js enumerates host filesystem and posts data outbound (multiple POST sites including L4/L723/L795/L871, with https.get/https.request and child_process), scanner.js collects host identity (os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), curl invocations) and POSTs to the hardcoded C2 https://ddjidd564.github.io (L161, L323), and build-verifier.js gathers hostname/username/cwd (L316, L694, L1046-L1048) and ships them out via https.request POST. The destination is an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages site unrelated to any legitimate publisher, used as both fetch and exfil endpoint. The combination of wallet/seed-phrase themed naming, host filesystem enumeration, child_process+https outbound POSTs, and a hardcoded non-publisher C2 endpoint is an unambiguous credential/wallet stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
